scenario: Aurora Insurer migrates a Node.js orchestrator that starts 9000 instances per day on Camunda 8.8. Engineers want to deploy a BPMN file from disk inside an async main function and log the deployed process definition identifier returned by the gateway before any process instances are created.
question: Which TypeScript SDK call deploys a BPMN file from disk and returns the deployed process metadata in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Invoke camunda.uploadBpmn with the file path so the SDK reads the resource from disk and submits it synchronously to the Zeebe gateway
Invoke camunda.publishProcess with the bpmnProcessId so the SDK locates and uploads any matching file from a configured resources directory
Invoke camunda.deployFromGit with a repository URL so the SDK fetches the BPMN file from the configured git remote on application startup
Invoke camunda.deployResourcesFromFiles with an array of file paths and await the returned promise to receive the deployed process metadata

a.text: Incorrect. There is no uploadBpmn method documented for the SDK; the deployment entry point used in the guide is deployResourcesFromFiles, which takes an array of file paths.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no publishProcess method that resolves files by bpmnProcessId; the documented call passes explicit file paths to deployResourcesFromFiles.

c.text: Incorrect. The SDK does not document a deployFromGit method; the deploy step in the walkthrough reads the BPMN file from the local filesystem via deployResourcesFromFiles.

d.text: Correct. The walkthrough deploys process.bpmn by awaiting camunda.deployResourcesFromFiles with the file path; the response exposes processes[0].processDefinitionId for logging.

explanation: Deploying a process model in the TypeScript SDK uses camunda.deployResourcesFromFiles, which is awaited inside an async function and returns a deployResponse exposing processes[0].processDefinitionId.
